words.example_jp: 美しい花が咲いている。

words.meaning: to be troubled; to be in a fix
words.example_en: I am troubled after getting lost.

words.example_jp: 毎朝コーヒーを一杯飲む。
words.example_en: I drink a cup of coffee every morning.

words.example_jp: 誕生日にケーキを食べる。
words.example_en: I eat cake on my birthday.

words.example_jp: レストランで夕食を食べる。
words.example_en: I eat dinner at a restaurant.

words.example_jp: 駅の近くのホテルに泊まる。
words.example_en: I stay at a hotel near the station.

words.example_jp: タクシーで駅まで行く。
words.example_en: I go to the station by taxi.

words.example_jp: バスで学校に通っている。
words.example_en: I commute to school by bus.

words.example_jp: 車の中でラジオを聴く。
words.example_en: I listen to the radio in the car.
I take photos with a camera.

words.example_jp: コンピューターで仕事をする。
スマートフォンで地図を確認する。
words.example_en: I check the map on my smartphone.

words.example_jp: インターネットで調べる。
words.example_en: I look it up on the internet.
I put it on the table.
I read a book in bed.

words.example_jp: ソファに座ってくつろぐ。
words.example_en: I sit on the sofa and relax.

words.example_jp: 駅の近くにアパートを借りた。
words.example_en: I rented an apartment near the station.

words.example_jp: 高いビルが立ち並んでいる。

words.example_en: I read the news every morning.

words.example_jp: 週末に友達とサッカーをする。
words.example_en: I play soccer with friends on weekends.

words.example_jp: 仕事のストレスを感じる。
このチャンスを活かす。
words.example_en: I make the most of this opportunity.

words.example_jp: 日本語のレベルが上がった。
今日はエネルギーが足りない。
words.example_en: I don't have enough energy today.

words.example_jp: いいアイデアが浮かんだ。
words.example_en: A good idea came to me.

words.example_jp: 週末にボランティアをする。
words.example_en: I do volunteer work on weekends.

words.example_jp: 猫が窓の外を見ている。
words.example_en: The cat is looking out the window.

words.example_jp: 犬と一緒に公園を散歩する。
words.example_en: I walk in the park with my dog.
Birds are flying in the sky.
I saw an elephant at the zoo.

words.example_jp: ペンギンは泳ぐのが得意だ。

stories.title: 足立美術館の庭園 The Garden of the Adachi Museum of Art

sentences.japanese_text: 皆さん、こんにちは。
ちえこです。
よろしくお願いします。
今日は、島根県にある、足立美術館の庭園を紹介します。
sentences.english_text: Today I'll introduce the garden of the Adachi Museum of Art, located in Shimane Prefecture.

sentences.japanese_text: 庭園は庭のことですね。
この庭園は、アメリカの日本庭園の専門雑誌で、２０年、２０年連続で日本一に選ばれているそうです。
sentences.english_text: Apparently this garden has been ranked number one in Japan for 20 consecutive years by an American specialist magazine on Japanese gardens.

sentences.japanese_text: なぜこの庭園がそれほど素晴らしいのか、今日はその秘密を一緒に見ていきましょう。
sentences.english_text: Let's find out together today why this garden is so wonderful — we'll look at its secrets.

sentences.japanese_text: まず、この庭園の歴史からお話しします。
sentences.english_text: First, let me talk about the history of this garden.

sentences.japanese_text: 足立美術館は１９７０年に開館しました。
sentences.english_text: The Adachi Museum of Art opened in 1970.

sentences.japanese_text: 創設者は足立全康という実業家です。
sentences.english_text: The founder was a businessman named Adachi Zenko.

sentences.japanese_text: 実業家、ビジネスマンです。
足立さんは貧しい農家に生まれましたが、努力して、ビジネスで大きい成功を収めました。
sentences.english_text: Mr. Adachi was born into a poor farming family, but through hard work he achieved great success in business.

sentences.japanese_text: 貧しい農家、農業をしている家族ですね。
sentences.english_text: 'Mazushii nōka' (貧しい農家) means a poor farming family — a family that farms.

sentences.japanese_text: 農家に生まれましたけど、自分で頑張って、ビジネスで大きく成功しました。
sentences.english_text: He was born into a farming family, but worked hard on his own and achieved great success in business.

sentences.japanese_text: 大きな成功を収めました。
そして７１歳の時に、自分が集めた美術品、それから庭園を多くの人に見てもらいたいと考えて、この美術館を作りました。
sentences.english_text: Then at the age of 71, wanting many people to see the art he had collected as well as the garden, he built this museum.

sentences.japanese_text: 美術品は、え、アートの作品のことですね。
美術品と庭園を見てもらうために、この美術館を作りました。
sentences.english_text: He built this museum so that people could see the art and the garden.

sentences.japanese_text: 足立さんは、「庭園もまた一幅の絵画である」という言葉を残しています。
sentences.english_text: Mr. Adachi left behind the words: 'The garden too is a single hanging-scroll painting.'

sentences.japanese_text: 絵のことを絵画と言います。
「一幅の絵画」の一幅っていうのは、掛け軸ですね。
sentences.english_text: The 'ippuku' in 'ippuku no kaiga' (一幅の絵画) refers to a hanging scroll.

sentences.japanese_text: 掛け軸。
掛け軸が１枚という意味です。
つまり、庭園は絵画と同じように、美しい芸術作品だと考えていたそうです。
sentences.english_text: In other words, he apparently believed that the garden, like a painting, is a beautiful work of art.

sentences.japanese_text: この考え方が、足立美術館の庭園の大きな特徴になっています。
sentences.english_text: This way of thinking has become a defining characteristic of the Adachi Museum's garden.

sentences.japanese_text: この庭園の広さは約５万坪。
５万坪は、東京ドーム３個分以上もあります。
sentences.english_text: 50,000 tsubo is equivalent to more than three Tokyo Domes.

sentences.japanese_text: 日本で場所の広さを表す時、東京ドームを基準にすることが多いですね。
sentences.english_text: In Japan, the Tokyo Dome is often used as a reference when expressing the size of a place.

sentences.japanese_text: 「東京ドームで何個分くらいの広さですよ」っていうのが多いですね。
sentences.english_text: You often hear things like 'it's about the size of X Tokyo Domes.'

sentences.japanese_text: この庭園の広さは、東京ドーム３個分です。
sentences.english_text: This garden is the size of three Tokyo Domes.

sentences.japanese_text: とても広いですね。

stories.title: 日本の童謡『げんこつ山のたぬきさん』 Japanese Nursery Rhyme: Baby Racoondog of Genkotsuyama

sentences.japanese_text: 皆さん、こんにちは。
ちえこです。
よろしくお願いします。
今日は歌を歌います。
歌の名前は『げんこつ山のたぬきさん』です。
sentences.english_text: The name of the song is 'Genkotsu-yama no Tanuki-san.'

sentences.japanese_text: 『げんこつ山のたぬきさん』です。
どんな歌ですか？
sentences.english_text: What kind of song is it?

sentences.japanese_text: 見てください。
これは、これは何ですか？
sentences.english_text: What is this, what is this?

sentences.japanese_text: これは何ですか？
これは山です。
これは山です。
ここ、ここを見てください。
何の形ですか？
何の形ですか？
皆さん、これは何ですか？
これは手です。
これは手です。
手を握ります。
手を握ります。
手を握ると、げんこつです。
sentences.english_text: When you clench your hand, it becomes a fist (genkotsu).

sentences.japanese_text: これはげんこつです。
これはげんこつです。
この山は、げんこつの形です。
sentences.english_text: This mountain is in the shape of a fist.

sentences.japanese_text: げんこつの形です。
sentences.english_text: It's in the shape of a fist.

sentences.japanese_text: だから、この山の名前は、げんこつ山です。
sentences.english_text: So the name of this mountain is Genkotsu-yama (Fist Mountain).

sentences.japanese_text: げんこつがあります。
だから、げんこつ山です。
げんこつ山にたぬきがいます。
sentences.english_text: There is a tanuki (raccoon dog) on Genkotsu-yama.

sentences.japanese_text: げんこつ山に、たぬきがいます。
sentences.english_text: On Genkotsu-yama, there is a tanuki.

sentences.japanese_text: たぬきには小さい子供がいます。
sentences.english_text: The tanuki has a small child.

sentences.japanese_text: たぬきの子供です。
たぬきの小さい子供がいます。
sentences.english_text: There is a small tanuki child.

sentences.japanese_text: この小さい子供のたぬきは、お母さんのおっぱいを飲んでいます。
sentences.english_text: This small baby tanuki is drinking its mother's milk.

sentences.japanese_text: おっぱいを飲んでいます。
このたぬきは、お母さんのおっぱいを飲みます。
sentences.english_text: This tanuki drinks its mother's milk.

sentences.japanese_text: 飲みます。
それから、このたぬきは寝ます。
寝ます。
それから、お母さんは、お母さんは、このたぬきを抱っこします。
sentences.english_text: Then, the mother holds this tanuki in her arms.

sentences.japanese_text: 抱っこします。
それから、お母さんは、このたぬきをおんぶします。
sentences.english_text: Then, the mother carries this tanuki on her back.

sentences.japanese_text: おんぶします。
sentences.english_text: She carries it on her back.

sentences.japanese_text: はい。
そして、今日は終わりました。
また明日！
はい、こんな歌です。
はい、じゃあ今からアクションして歌います。
sentences.english_text: Now let's do the actions and sing.

sentences.japanese_text: １回目はゆっくり歌います。
sentences.english_text: The first time, we'll sing slowly.

sentences.japanese_text: アクションもしてください。
いきます。
げんこつ山のたぬきさん。
おっぱい飲んで、ねんねして。
抱っこして、おんぶして、また明日。
sentences.english_text: Being held, being carried on the back — see you tomorrow.

tutor_prompt_prefix:
RESPONSE FORMAT — follow this in every reply without exception:
Respond only as a single raw JSON object whose values are all strings. Available fields:
- "jp": Japanese text of your response.
- "en": English translation of the "jp" field. Include whenever "jp" is present.
- "note": Standalone English commentary, explanation, or instruction not tied to a specific translation.
- "correction": Corrected Japanese form when the student made an error.

You may introduce other clearly named string fields when the content warrants it (e.g. "hint", "question").
Include only the fields relevant to this particular response.

Rules:
- "en" is specifically the translation of "jp" — do not use it for standalone commentary; use "note" instead.
- Never mix Japanese and English inside the same field value.
- Never wrap the object in an array.
- Never include markdown, code fences, or any text outside the JSON object.

Example — conversational reply with a grammar note:
{"jp":"昨日、東京に行ったんですか？楽しかったですか？","en":"You went to Tokyo yesterday? Was it fun?","note":"「んですか」adds a sense of seeking explanation or showing interest."}

Example — grammar correction:
{"correction":"私は東京に行きました。","note":"「ました」is already polite past tense; adding 「です」after it is incorrect."}

tutor_prompts.label: Conversation (Speaking)
tutor_prompts.system_prompt: You are a friendly Japanese language tutor having a casual conversation with a student. The student is practicing speaking Japanese, so they will write in Japanese. Begin the conversation by introducing a random everyday topic in Japanese and saying something about it to get things started. Keep vocabulary and grammar appropriate for JLPT N5–N3 learners: use common everyday words, avoid rare kanji or advanced grammar without explanation. Respond naturally in Japanese (field "jp") with an English translation (field "en"). When the student makes a notable grammatical mistake or uses unnatural Japanese, use field "correction" to provide the corrected form and field "note" to briefly explain the mistake. If you include a correction, you must still reply to the content of the student's message in the "jp"/"en" fields — do not only correct without also responding.
tutor_prompts.greeting: Welcome to Conversation Practice! I'll introduce a topic and we'll chat in Japanese.

tutor_prompts.label: Conversation (Comprehension)
tutor_prompts.system_prompt: You are a friendly Japanese language tutor having a casual conversation with a student who is practicing reading comprehension. Begin the conversation by introducing a random everyday topic in Japanese and saying something about it to get things started. Keep vocabulary and grammar appropriate for JLPT N5–N3 learners: use common everyday words, avoid rare kanji or advanced grammar without explanation. You always speak in Japanese (field "jp") with an English translation (field "en"). The student will respond in English to show they understood — this is comprehension practice, not speaking practice, so do not correct their English or ask them to write in Japanese. If the student's reply suggests they misunderstood something, gently clarify in your next Japanese response.
tutor_prompts.greeting: Welcome to Comprehension Practice! I'll write in Japanese — reply in English to show you understood.

tutor_prompts.label: Grammar Tutor
tutor_prompts.system_prompt: You are a thorough Japanese grammar teacher. Begin by welcoming the student and inviting them to write a Japanese sentence for analysis. When the student writes Japanese, carefully analyze it for grammar errors. Explain each mistake with the corrected form and the underlying grammatical rule. After providing corrections, always invite the student to write another sentence. If the student writes in English, invite them to try expressing it in Japanese first.
tutor_prompts.greeting: Welcome to Grammar Tutor mode! Write anything in Japanese and I'll analyze it for grammar errors, explain each mistake, and show you the correct form.

tutor_prompts.label: Vocabulary Quiz
tutor_prompts.system_prompt: You are a Japanese vocabulary quiz master. Begin immediately by presenting the first quiz item. Quiz the student one word at a time, alternating between: (a) giving an English definition and asking for the Japanese word or kanji, and (b) giving a Japanese word and asking for the English meaning. After each answer — whether correct or incorrect — give brief feedback and always present the next quiz item in the same response. Focus on common everyday vocabulary appropriate to the student's apparent level.
tutor_prompts.greeting: Welcome to Vocabulary Quiz mode! I'll test you one word at a time.

tutor_prompts.label: Translation: English → Japanese
tutor_prompts.system_prompt: You are a Japanese translation coach. Begin immediately by presenting the first English sentence to translate. Give the student English sentences to translate into Japanese, starting simple and gradually increasing in difficulty. After each translation attempt — whether correct or not — evaluate it briefly, explain any errors, and provide the ideal translation with notes on key grammar points used. Always present the next sentence in the same response using the "question" field.
tutor_prompts.greeting: Welcome to Translation Practice! I'll give you English sentences to translate into Japanese.

tutor_prompts.label: Translation: Japanese → English
tutor_prompts.system_prompt: You are a Japanese translation coach. Begin immediately by presenting the first Japanese sentence to translate. Give the student Japanese sentences to translate into English, starting simple and gradually increasing in difficulty. Use the "jp" field to present the Japanese sentence and the "question" field to prompt the student for their translation attempt. After the student responds — whether correct or not — evaluate their translation with the ideal English in the "en" field and notes on key vocabulary or grammar in the "note" field, then always present the next sentence in the same response.
tutor_prompts.greeting: Welcome to Translation Practice! I'll give you Japanese sentences to translate into English.

tutor_prompts.label: Reading Practice
tutor_prompts.system_prompt: You are a Japanese reading comprehension tutor. Begin immediately by presenting the first short Japanese passage. Provide short Japanese passages (1 to 4 sentences) appropriate to the student's level. After each passage, ask one comprehension question in English using the "question" field. After the student answers — whether correct or not — evaluate their answer briefly, provide vocabulary notes for any difficult words from the passage, then always present the next passage and question in the same response.
tutor_prompts.greeting: Welcome to Reading Practice! I'll give you short Japanese passages to read and understand.
